  "method_chain": [
    {
      "id": "step-01",
      "depends_on": [],
      "step_type": "input_catalog",
      "summary": "APOGEE DR14 catalog of stellar parameters, chemical abundances, and radial velocities for ~176,000 red giant stars.",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-02",
      "depends_on": ["step-01"],
      "step_type": "quality_filter",
      "summary": "Apply quality cuts: 3550 < Teff < 4200 K, S/N > 70, no ASPCAPBAD flag, [Fe/H] > -1.2.",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": [],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex",
          "start_line": 166,
          "end_line": 175,
          "context": "Sample definition cuts for the APOGEE MW sample."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-03",
      "depends_on": ["step-02"],
      "step_type": "sample_selection",
      "summary": "K-means clustering with k=16 in 6D chemical space ([O/Fe], [(C+N)/Fe], [Mg/Fe], [Al/Fe], [Mn/Fe], [Ni/Fe]) to select Sgr-like stars, yielding 68 Sgr stream candidates.",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": [],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex",

          "context": "Definition of 6D chemical space for clustering."

        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex",
          "start_line": 230,
          "end_line": 234,
          "context": "Choosing k=16 and tuning the clustering algorithm."
        },
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex",
          "start_line": 248,
          "end_line": 249,
          "context": "Result of clustering: 68 Sgr stream candidates."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-04",
      "depends_on": [],
      "step_type": "input_catalog",
      "summary": "Gaia Data Release 2 proper motions.",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": [],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex",
          "start_line": 179,
          "end_line": 179,
          "context": "Gaia DR2 proper motions supplement APOGEE data."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-05",
      "depends_on": ["step-03", "step-04"],
      "step_type": "cross_match",
      "summary": "Cross-match APOGEE Sgr candidates with Gaia DR2 using TOPCAT nearest-neighbor matching within 1 arcsec.",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": [],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex",
          "start_line": 179,
          "end_line": 180,
          "context": "Matching APOGEE and Gaia using TOPCAT, identifying 62/68 candidates with Gaia matches."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-06",
      "depends_on": ["step-01"],
      "step_type": "distance_estimation",
      "summary": "Adopt spectro-photometric distances from the DR14 value-added catalog (NMSU_dist) using methods of Hayden et al. (2014).",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": [],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex",
          "start_line": 175,
          "end_line": 176,
          "context": "Distances derived from methods described in Hayden et al. (2014) and available in the DR14 VAC."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-07",
      "depends_on": ["step-01"],
      "step_type": "radial_velocity_measurement",
      "summary": "Radial velocities from APOGEE DR14.",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": [],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex",

          "end_line": 162,

          "start_line": 179,
          "end_line": 179,
          "context": "Supplement APOGEE radial velocities with Gaia proper motions."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-08",
      "depends_on": [],
      "step_type": "solar_position_and_motion",
      "summary": "Adopt solar Galactocentric radius R0 = 8.0 kpc, circular velocity v_circ = 220 km/s, and solar peculiar motion from Schoenrich et al. (2010).",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": [],

          "start_line": 181,
          "end_line": 182,
          "context": "Assumed solar position and motion for velocity calculations."
        }

    },
    {
      "id": "step-09",
      "depends_on": ["step-05", "step-06", "step-07", "step-08"],
      "step_type": "velocity_calculation",
      "summary": "Compute Galactocentric Cartesian positions and total space velocity (3D) using galpy with observed proper motions, distances, and solar parameters.",

      "outputs": [],
      "parameters": [],

          "start_line": 181,
          "end_line": 182,
          "context": "Use galpy to initialize orbits at current position and velocity, adopting a left-handed Galactic Cartesian system."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-10",
      "depends_on": [],
      "step_type": "galactic_potential_model",
      "summary": "Use two Milky Way gravitational potentials: MWPotential2014 (Bovy 2015) and the Law & Majewski (2010) potential.",

          "start_line": 300,

    {

      "step_type": "orbit_integration",
      "summary": "Integrate orbits forward and backward for 1.5 Gyr using galpy/gala with both potentials, and compute orbital parameters (eccentricity, apocenter, pericenter).",
